hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 complementarity while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions. Mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric fit in the SSTR2-specific ECL3 pocket, increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that the observed Δmargin is limited by conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Targeted mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 are expected to improve selectivity by reducing off-target binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: To specifically target non-pharmacophore positions that are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are critical for disrupting conserved-pocket contacts without affecting the pharmacophore core.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining the structural integrity and pharmacophore of the native SST-14.
